title: About SysBio FAIRplex
body:
SysBio FAIRplex

SysBio FAIRplex, a FAIR PLatform for EXploration of Systems Biology, aims to transform multi-modal, multi-tissue, and multi-source data into insights for human disease. Our centralized portal provides access to cross-program summary-level analyses and visualizations from harmonized data from the Accelerating Medicines Partnership® (AMP®) ecosystem to yield scientific insights.

SysBio FAIRplex is part of the Venture Program SysBio Initiative funded by the United States National Institutes of Health (NIH) Common Fund under Award Number OT2OD037975. We leverage FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable and Reusable) principles to maximize the value of data and resources generated by the AMP programs. Our major goals are to:

  • Harmonize data from across the AMPs to uncover new pathways for inflammation
  • Support AI-readiness with harmonized, interoperable data on the same scale
  • Decrease the activation energy required for innovative research
  • Enable rapid hypothesis testing through the portal and provide a streamlined FAIR platform for data analysis
  • Expand the number and types of researchers who can find and access data within the AMP ecosystem
  • Increase the number and variety of tools that can be used to analyze the data
  • Enable “big-N” science by combining silos into larger virtual cohorts
  • Connect the AMP ecosystem to the larger NIH and global data and research ecosystem
  • Enable seamless, rapid, secure cloud-based computing

 

Current Offerings

Our initial platform provides a publicly accessible entrypoint into the AMP ecosystem, through which users can learn which AMP data are available and being harmonized by FAIRplex, how to access AMP data directly, and how we define terms within FAIRplex. We also provide summary-level analyses and visualizations from harmonized transcriptomic and genetic data across multiple AMP programs. Here are our initial features:

  • Gene Expression Browser: interactive visualizations of differential gene expression within human tissues derived from the AMP programs.
  • Data Summary Browser: summaries of the demographics and phenotypes within cohorts from the AMP-AD, AMP-CMD, AMP-PDRD, and AMP-RA/SLE programs for which FAIRplex has generated harmonized RNA-sequencing data.
  • Genetic Studies: analysis outputs and visualizations of summary statistics from case versus case Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS) from individuals with Alzheimer's Disease (AD) from AMP-AD versus Parkinson's Disease and Related Disorders (PDRD) from AMP-PDRD.

 

About Us

SysBio FAIRplex is developed by a multidisciplinary team with expertise in security, governance, data and life sciences, and technical implementation. Our leadership team hails from Verily, Technome, DataTecnica, Sage Bionetworks, Vanderbilt Institute for Clinical and Translational Research (VICTR), Northwestern University, and the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard. We have collectively built and managed platforms for related projects including AMP AD, AMP AIM, AMP CMD, the Global Parkinson’s Genetics Program (GP2), AMP PDRD, AMP RA/SLE, and AMP T2D. 

 

Coming Soon

SysBio FAIRplex is a nascent resource that is actively developing new features. We invite requests for new collaborations, visualizations, or portal features at support@sysbio-fairplex.org. Our platform will grow to include summary-level representations from additional datasets and data types generated by AMP and non-AMP programs. Future offerings will expand data harmonization efforts across the AMPs, with a key focus on bulk and single-cell RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) data.
